entities.name: FUSARIUM OXYSPORUM F. SP. CICERI
entities.description: A systemic fungus that causes Fusarium wilt in chickpea and can survive in soil and crop residues

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: The host plant (Cicer arietinum) affected by Fusarium wilt

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A seed- and soil-borne disease of economic importance affecting chickpea

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: The specific type of soil used to study the survival of the fungus

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant remains including leaflets, terminal branches, stems, and roots where the fungus survives

entities.name: FLOODED SOIL
entities.description: A soil condition that limits the survival of the pathogen within host tissue

entities.name: WET SOIL
entities.description: A soil condition that allows the pathogen to survive for over 200 days

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: An agricultural practice studied to determine its influence on wilt incidence

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: An agricultural practice studied to determine its influence on wilt incidence

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop used in the study of crop rotation and intercropping

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A crop used in the study of crop rotation and intercropping

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A crop used in the study of crop rotation and intercropping

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTRE PATANCHERU
entities.description: The location where the wilt sick plot study was conducted

entities.name: JG 62
entities.description: A highly susceptible cultivar of chickpea used in the study
